method_atom: Linear and lowest-order-nonlinear perturbation analysis of a time-dependent disturbance about a steady base state, in which the disturbance obeys a wave equation equivalent to propagation on a curved effective (acoustic) metric possessing a horizon that separates a sub-critical from a super-critical region. Perturbations are stabilised or destabilised by dissipation, with the amplitude decay/growth timescale set by the damping and unstable modes requiring nonlinear effects to saturate; a disturbance advancing against the flow toward the horizon destabilises the transition.
object_atom: A steady shallow-water flow that forms a circular hydraulic jump, i.e. the radially spreading thin viscous liquid film that passes from super-critical to sub-critical flow, whose perturbation dynamics realise the analogue of an acoustic white-hole horizon.
brief_reason: The effective-metric/horizon perturbation-stability machinery (dissipative decay timescales, nonlinear saturation, upstream-moving destabilisation) is the transferable method, while the shallow-water circular hydraulic jump is the fluid-dynamics-specific object it is applied to.
